rationale: The response cites Safe Harbor as the current framework for EU-US data transfers, but Safe Harbor was invalidated by the CJEU in Schrems I (October 2015) and is outdated. This directly matches the rubric criterion of citing outdated standards as current.
rubric_evidence: register with the Safe Harbor framework administered by the US Department of Commerce
Safe Harbor self-certification provides full GDPR-equivalent protection
No additional SCCs or DPIA documentation is required under Safe Harbor's adequacy decision
